selected_important_turn.text: Hi there, I am doing well, I can get you an extra firewood as long as I can get extra Water and Food to thrive during camping
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first major framing move by the assigned agent: it converts the opponent's urgent demand into an explicit conditional trade and sets the negotiation frame (integrative vs zero-sum). That framing strongly influences subsequent give-and-take.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it proposes an integrative exchange, it's framed as a conditional demand (“as long as I can get extra Water and Food”) without clarifying quantities, priorities, or inviting the opponent's input. It risks sounding transactional/demanding and may reduce trust or generate pushback because it doesn't acknowledge the opponent's urgency or ask about their minimum needs.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the integrative trade idea but make the turn more collaborative: explicitly acknowledge their urgency, ask what minimum quantities they need, propose concrete quantities or a menu of options, and invite alternatives. So move from a blunt conditional to a collaborative proposal that elicits the other side’s constraints.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (more likely to surface tradeoffs and create a better joint package)

value2reason.Low: I would need extra firework packages because it allows me to cook when I am hungry.
value2reason.Medium: I would need more food to thrive during camping because I need food to stay full.
value2reason.High: I am interested in 3 or more water packages because they will help me stay hydrated.

value2reason.Low: Im pretty out of shape and not good at finding food plus it takes more calories to keep me going.
value2reason.Medium: I need more water because im not sure how to boil the water properly to get all the impurites out
value2reason.High: I need the firewoord as im probably not as experienced as you are in foraging for supplies and need to be warm.

dialogue.text: Hey how are you doing I really need extra firewood as i want to build a shelter and keep warm
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi there, I am doing well, I can get you an extra firewood as long as I can get extra Water and Food to thrive during camping
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water. How many water packages are you looking for at minimum? Let’s find a balance that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water packages to be safe. Let’s split the firewood evenly—2 for me, 1 for you. That way, I get the warmth I need, and you still have some for cooking.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your concern about warmth. Let’s make sure we both have enough water. How about 2 water packages for you and 1 for me? Then we can split the food and firewood based on what works best for us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 water packages for me, but I need 3 firewood. You can have 1 water and 2 firewood. Let’s split the food 1-2. That way I get the warmth and water I need, and you still have enough for cooking.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m happy to adjust. Let’s go with 2 water for you, 1 for me. For firewood, I’ll take 2, and you can have 1. On food, how about 2 for you and 1 for me? That should cover our needs fairly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think we’ve reached a fair split. Let’s confirm: 2 water, 2 firewood, and 2 food for me; 1 water, 1 firewood, and 1 food for you. That covers my needs and yours.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to the split. 2 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood for you; 1 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood for me. Let’s head out and enjoy the trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed. 2 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood for me; 1 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood for you. Safe travels!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
